steps.title: Write in standard form and recognize the quadratic formula structure
steps.content:
Rewrite $6x^2-11x+c=4$ as $6x^2-11x+(c-4)=0$. By the quadratic formula, both solutions take the form:
$$x = \frac{11 \pm \sqrt{121 - 24(c-4)}}{12} = \frac{11 \pm \sqrt{217 - 24c}}{12}$$

steps.title: Match the given solution to extract the discriminant
steps.content:
The given solution is $x = \frac{11-\sqrt{601}}{12}$, which matches our formula with the minus sign and discriminant 601. Therefore:
$$217 - 24c = 601$$
$$-24c = 384$$
$$c = -16$$
